# Transcrição: A_Year_and_a_Half_of_Videos

- **Fonte:** A_Year_and_a_Half_of_Videos
- **Duração:** 01:29
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:29]

So today marks a year and a half since I started publishing at least one video every single day. Now I realize that's a pretty insane accomplishment and I am super proud of it. But what makes me even prouder is that two years ago I wasn't creating any videos at all. So if you've been meaning to get started but you just can't imagine yourself doing it, just know that that was me a couple years ago. I'd say the most important lesson that I've learned along the way is to ignore the vanity metrics. Now of course they can be helpful. You've got to measure something but views and new followers, those numbers come and go and they're up and down, they'll drive you crazy. I guarantee I would have quit doing this multiple times if I tied accomplishment to those numbers. Instead I know that all this is worthwhile because I consistently hear from people who book one-on-ones, who sign up for my membership, and they tell me they did that because they see my videos every day and they feel like they know me. That's powerful. A secondary benefit is that this process keeps me sharp. It forces me to come up with new ideas every day and then I can leverage this content in lots of different ways including my email list and on my website. It's been fun and it's been challenging at times but most importantly it's been rewarding and 100% worth it. So I hope you found value in my videos and I'm not stopping anytime soon. Thanks for watching.
